response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and backend engineer with a career built primarily on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, falling drastically short of the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows required. He has no demonstrated experience with financial-domain systems — capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking platforms — and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, STL, or relevant messaging frameworks. The RIDE Capital role sounds adjacent but was a Node/TypeScript/GraphQL engagement at a German fintech startup, not a capital markets or fixed income trading system.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly across web, mobile, embedded, and data domains
Strong DevOps and CI/CD culture — GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development mentioned across multiple roles
Evidence of low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months in 2014–2015 and a thesis project) — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required
Zero demonstrable fixed income, capital markets, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge
No mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, STL, Kafka, or any other technology listed as mandatory or strongly desirable for this role

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
